pr.title: Bring in Chrisnonymous's work on ftNess and match some additional functions
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Imported Chrisnonymous's ftNess work and moved an initial block of Ness fighter code from assembly into C, while leaving several harder routines as inline asm. The PR added src/melee/ft/chara/ftness.c and ftness.h, wired ftness.o into obj_files.mk and e_files.mk, added missing Fighter field x224C, and promoted several unknown function declarations in include/functions.h to typed prototypes. It also renamed assembly references from lbl_801148F8 to func_801148F8 so remaining asm could call the new C symbol.
what_changed: Removed roughly the first 300 lines of ftNess assembly from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftness.s and replaced them with a new C translation unit at src/melee/ft/chara/ftness.c.
Added C implementations for straightforward ftNess routines including func_801147C0, func_801148F8, func_8011493C, func_8011495C, func_80114A48, func_80114A90, func_80114AD8, and func_80114B6C.

Kept skipped or difficult routines such as ftNess_OnLoad, func_80114968, and func_80114B2C as inline asm inside ftness.c, matching the PR body note: "Inlined some things that I didn't feel like matching right now, since Chris skipped functions."
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftness.h with ftNessAttributes, prototypes for the new ftNess C symbols, and includes for HSD_GObj and Fighter.
Added Fighter.x224C at offset 0x224C in src/melee/ft/fighter.h because func_801147C0 clears x2240, x2244, x2248, x222C, and x224C.

Updated include/functions.h with typed prototypes for func_800704F0(struct _HSD_GObj*, s32, f32), func_80114CF4(struct _HSD_GObj*), func_80115AF8(struct _HSD_GObj*), func_80116F38(struct _HSD_GObj*), and func_80117E60(struct _HSD_GObj*).
Added ftness.o to both obj_files.mk and e_files.mk so the new source file is built while the remaining ftNess asm object still exists.
Changed remaining ftNess assembly references from lbl_801148F8 to func_801148F8 where the address is stored into Fighter callback fields x21E4 and x21DC.
smart_moves: Used a mixed C plus inline-asm migration instead of blocking the entire ftNess source port on hard-to-match routines.
Converted the former local label lbl_801148F8 into a real C symbol func_801148F8 and updated remaining asm address loads, preserving callback pointer behavior while allowing the routine to live in C.
Added typed external prototypes only for functions needed by the new C code, reducing reliance on unknown declarations without over-naming unrelated APIs.
Modeled the only needed portion of Ness special attributes as u8 data[0x94] plus f32 unk94, enough for func_8011495C to access offset 0x94 without inventing the full attribute layout.
Kept the original asm body for loop-heavy or skipped functions, including the OnLoad copy loop and item-state switch logic, which avoided speculative C restructures.
decomp_lessons: When a character file contains a mix of easy wrappers and difficult routines, partial decompilation can still retire asm by moving matched functions to C and embedding unresolved routines as asm in the new source file.
Callback labels referenced by remaining assembly must be promoted or renamed consistently when their target becomes a C function symbol.
Struct work can be incremental: add verified Fighter fields and minimal per-character attribute structs at known offsets rather than guessing complete layouts.
Build-system wiring matters for split asm/C character files: add the new src object while retaining the remaining asm object until the whole file is translated.
Header prototypes for cross-file calls are part of matching work; typed HSD_GObj and Fighter signatures help the C compile cleanly and document call conventions.
naming_conventions: New character files follow lowercase path naming: src/melee/ft/chara/ftness.c and src/melee/ft/chara/ftness.h.
The character OnLoad symbol uses the existing mixed-case style ftNess_OnLoad.
Unidentified functions retained address-based names such as func_801147C0, func_801148F8, and func_80114B6C.
The new special attributes typedef uses ftNessAttributes with an unknown field named unk94 at offset 0x94.
Fighter struct fields follow offset-derived names such as x2240, x2244, x2248, and newly added x224C.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used inline asm with nofralloc for functions not yet matched in C, preserving exact instruction sequences inside ftness.c.
Used #pragma push, #pragma peephole on, and #pragma pop around groups of simple C functions, indicating compiler scheduling/peephole behavior was needed for matching.
Matched simple wrapper functions directly: func_801148F8 calls func_80115AF8, func_80116F38, func_80117E60, and func_80114CF4 in sequence; func_8011493C only calls func_8011B0F8.
Matched simple field access in func_8011495C by casting Fighter.x2D4_specialAttributes to ftNessAttributes and returning unk94.
Preserved difficult assembly loops for copying 0x1B iterations of data in ftNess_OnLoad and func_80114B2C instead of forcing an uncertain C memcpy-like form.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the PR slice.
The only rationale text available was the author note that some functions were left inline because Chris skipped them and the author did not want to match them yet.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully decompile ftNess_OnLoad, func_80114968, or func_80114B2C after this inline-asm bridge.
Search for later renames of ftNessAttributes.unk94 to identify the real Ness attribute field name.
Search for later changes to Fighter fields x2240, x2244, x2248, x224C to understand their callback/item-related semantics.
Look for PRs that replace address-based ftNess function names with action or special-move names.
